title: Technical Diagrams
description: Technical diagrams of a project: architecture views, operational workflows, dataflows (also for data-privacy records), sequence diagrams. One document holds many diagrams, like the mockup holds many pages. It is the source of truth: the diagram.html export is generated by the backend from this JSON. nodes[].pos is the manual position on the frontend canvas, set by the user dragging — never generated by the agent for new nodes, and it must be preserved (matched by id) when the agent updates the document.

title.description: Readable title of the document (e.g. 'Larkfield — architecture and flows').

date.description: Date of the last substantial update, YYYY-MM-DD format.

status.description: Editorial state: 'draft' while it is refined in chat (citing elements by id), 'confirmed' once the user validated it. A document without 'status' is treated as a draft.

diagrams.description: The diagrams. Each is rendered on its own canvas/page, in this order.

id.description: Unique diagram identifier within the document (e.g. 'arch-overview', 'wf-visit'). Used to cite the diagram in chat.

kind.description: What the diagram shows, which decides how it is laid out and drawn. 'architecture': systems and integrations (boxes and groups). 'workflow': an operational process (start/end, steps, decisions). 'dataflow': how data moves between actors and stores (also for data-privacy mapping). 'sequence': ordered message exchange between participants — nodes are the participants (lifelines, in array order), edges are the messages in array order.

title.description: Readable title shown above the diagram.

notes.description: Optional caption rendered under the title: scope, assumptions, what is out of the picture.

groups.description: Optional containers drawn as labelled boxes around their nodes: network zones, trust boundaries, departments, sites. May be nested via parent.

id.description: Unique group identifier within the diagram, referenced by nodes[].group and by parent.

label.description: Readable name of the zone (e.g. 'DMZ', 'Site A — clinic', 'Third parties').

parent.description: Reference to another groups[].id when this group is nested inside it.

nodes.description: The elements of the diagram. For 'sequence' diagrams the array order is the left-to-right order of the lifelines.

id.description: Unique node identifier within the diagram, referenced by edges (e.g. 'api', 'crm', 'step-triage'). Short readable slug.

label.description: Text shown in the box (e.g. 'Practice API', 'Reception triage').

class.description: Semantic class, which decides shape and colour through the theme — never pick colours directly. People and systems: 'actor' (a person or role), 'frontend', 'backend', 'service', 'database', 'storage', 'queue', 'external' (a third-party system), 'security' (auth, gateway, firewall). Workflow steps: 'start', 'end', 'process' (a step), 'decision' (a branch, drawn as a diamond), 'document' (a produced artefact), 'manual' (a step done by hand, outside any system).

group.description: Reference to groups[].id — the container this node sits in.

desc.description: One-line detail shown small under the label (e.g. 'PostgreSQL 16', 'daily batch').

pos.description: Manual position on the frontend canvas, set by the user dragging the node. If absent, the layout is automatic. Never written by the agent for new nodes; preserved by id on updates.

edges.description: The connections. For 'sequence' diagrams the array order is the vertical order of the messages.

label.description: Text on the arrow: the protocol, the data exchanged, the condition of a workflow branch (e.g. 'REST/JSON', 'yes', 'invoice PDF').

style.description: 'solid' (default) for direct calls and normal flow; 'dashed' for async, batch, or return messages.
